reviews.review_description:
To say that Solara One is literally Out of this World would be too cliche but OMG!
Minutes are turning into hours without realising.
After work, I just floated over the Earth and suddenly 20 mins had passed while I unwound from the day.
I am still exploring and finding more exciting experiences. 
Awesome graphics and physics. 

From a wellbeing or mindfulness position, this ticks all the box I need.

I cannot wait to see what is added in future updates.

It is stunning for sure. Breathtaking might be , for me , taking it too far .

I did experience genuine vertigo looking out over the edge of the spaceport , watching the clouds and actually wobbled uneasily while trying to walk along the edge of the platform.
So the sense of spatial reality is there.

Out in space , actually less so . Sorry devs.

It’s the “depth” perception I seem to lack when surrounded by a star field. Don’t quite feel “ in the moment”.

Stars , planet and nubula or superb and the hint of rotation does heighten the realism.
( Maybe our own solar system as part of the updated tours ? )

I have used the ISS VR app since its launch ( pardon the pun ) and maybe this has blunted my WOW factor ?  

Maybe real space is actually like this , stars so so far away over unfathomable vast distances that we don’t actually experience distance / volume the same as on earth with a horizon ?

Perhaps we need an actual astronauts perspective on this  ?

The spaceport , space station etc are superb and the shiny toy robot a delight.

It’s great as a showcase experience and we need more of these in the current VR climate , so thank you developers.

Fab!

What an incredible experience. This is easily one of the most relaxing things I’ve done with my Quest. It’s clear the creator put a great deal of thought and effort into this app—it truly shows. The sensation of floating in space feels remarkably real, making it perfect for meditation or simply soaking in the view. Thank you for creating such a stunning and immersive experience.

reviews.review_title: This is a must. Visually stunning,
reviews.review_description:
STUNNING
This app is one of the reasons I got into VR in the first place.

Awesome scenes, beautiful vistas, and the vastness of space feels so close in this.  Let alone the gravity effects 👌

Especially loving the little secret 'Easter eggs' hidden throughout as well.

Are they intentional, quirks or something else. I won't name any here as I don't wanna spoil people discovering them.

Can't wait for more from these guys.

Thank you chaps.
Awesome work.

This is my kind of explore app. I just wish I could beam down to the planet surfaces for some of the planet locations. At Earth it would be great to have a private beach to beam down to. I AM looking forward to the arboretum update! Very nice to have a space location to go to. I'm really disappointed meta got rid of home environments, so games like this are important additions. We need more like this.

When I want to get away from everything, I go to Solara One. the most beautiful and immersive game. I truly feel like I've been there  Congrats you guys are amazing, keep it coming! and thank you for this 💎!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your positive vibes! The Arboretum is going to great, looking forward to sharing it :)

reviews.review_description: World needs less fighting, more Zen, great job. Love to hang some family pictures in a personal state room. Multi player so I can sucker people into my state room to look at my family pictures.

Great experience in vr. Wandering, watching and being entranced by things. The simplicity of a metallic articulated doll moving in zero gravity, mixed with fluidity of the neck movement being so organic that I felt happy just watching it. Happily recommended.

If you ever wondered what it's like to be in space, this is it, total chill out and WOW! This is an amazing experience by one dev! I just hope the dev makes an actual game. For the price of a coffee and cake, get this help the dev to create more! Zen!

reviews.review_title: Now one of the sharpest apps on Quest 3
reviews.review_description:
First of all, the application can help you relax. It is not a classical game.

I had critizised the app before for not using a low rendertarget and not utilizing the power of the XR2 Gen 2 from Quest 3. The developer has updated the app and added free conent. Now the app is running at roughly 3.2k x 3.3k rendertarget. This is very high for the Quest 3 and attacks Red Matter 2 for the highest rendertarget. The new state room also looks impressiv. If this state room would be available as a Meta homeenvironment, maybe i would switch to that.

Great VR experience - this does what a VR app should do - 1) makes you say "wow" and 2) tricks your brain for a second, like "wait, is this really happening?". This is really uncommon, especially with standalone. Apparently you have to be some kind of optimization wizard to avoid blurry textures or cartoony graphics. This app has crisp clear skyboxes, nice cloud effects, good wind sounds, real immersive stuff.

Now this is described as a relaxing meditation experience, but I personally find being high up in the clouds or floating in space terrifying. It's more like immersion therapy for me. I like to throw the meditation crystals over the edge and watch them fall into the abyss - makes for a nice sinking feeling in the pit of my stomach. Also I keep checking if I can fall off the platforms by walking forward - what's wrong with me?

I read in the discord there are no plans for multiplayer - but I think that would work really well for this - even if it's easier to just use meta avatars. People need nicer hang-out spots than Horizon Worlds can offer. Would be cool to do a co-op spacewalk collecting space debris, or something.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review and positive feedback. Glad you enjoyed the feeling of true immersion that the experience was designed for. I'll keep multiplayer in mind, perhaps a new lounge area on the space station where people could hang out with friends to chill out a bit...

And yes optimization wizard indeed, many years of hard work that nearly melted my brain a number of times...

reviews.review_description:
This app is a planetarium on steroids. I’m truly blown away by the graphics, realism and entire experience of exploring these destinations. I rarely write reviews but I wanted to truly compliment the developers for this masterpiece and recommend this game to anyone who is interested in exploring space. 
I’m amazed by the whole experience. The personal space station, the variety of destinations and ways to explore them, the feeling of floating in space. I also have to mention, the destination Cyrus Nebula is truly a piece of art. I am blown away every time I see it. This app is an absolute gem for only 10 bucks.

This isn't a game, but more of a relaxation and meditation destination, with absolutely gorgeous graphics (some of the best I've seen in VR) and  cool opportunities for interactions and exploration. Right from the beginning on the jungle dock, it is immersive and soothing and provides visual and aural beauty worth savoring (and when you get out into space - holy WOW it is gorgeous). The meditation prompts and music are great if you want them, but you don't have to use them (the app has an excellent level of customizability). And I can't frickin' wait until the bio-habitat is added - it will be a great addition to an already awesome experience. (I am amazed this is the product of a solo dev - kudos!)
My only disappointment was I wish Solara One was a multi-player app so I could share the experience at the same time with my non VR friends on my other headset.

I love this, its exactly what I want in VR
first time falling asleep in VR looking up at the world in lounge too. wonderful experience

we need interaction though.. bed needs to be able to be slept in, coffee machine work, youtube music interface in stateroom. things like this would mean so much

Not a game, but a relaxing experience, and it's improved significantly with the latest updates. The graphics are now better on the Quest 3, and there are objects to interact with. Very nice. An unfinished room suggests that further updates are coming, which would be great since the overall scope is manageable. It would be fantastic if, in addition to teleporting to galaxies, there was a kind of "travel mode" with procedurally generated content, giving the feeling of exploring an unknown universe.
Solara One is a beautiful and relaxing experience. I really enjoy exploring the environment and the new hand physics update. It would be amazing to visit more planets.

After reading all the praise regarding the graphics I expected much better environments than this.

If the sole criteria by which you judge graphics quality is resolution and you feel that QGO's resolution boost is the cure for all graphics woes, then you should be very pleased with this because you won't even need your QGO if you're running on the Quest 3.

I place far more value on art direction, (design, modeling, animation, lighting, effects, etc.), than I do on resolution. High resolution is at the very bottom of my list, just a "nice to have" feature.

The graphics in the beginning are sharp and the frame rate is silky smooth. The art direction unfortunately is very generic and bland.

I was still really looking forward to getting out in space, until I saw the airlock. The surfaces are rendered with lumpy looking flat textures with the lighting and surface details baked in.  Once you're drifting out beyond the airlock the space station is equally disappointing.

I really couldn't get immersed so I'm requesting a refund.

However judging by the popularity of QGO most people should definitely give this a try.

developer_response.body:
The textures in this experience are 16k, the highest supported by the quest hardware. Our recent visual quality update makes QGO unnecessary: the native visual fidelity is on par with top quest titles like Red Matter 2.

Here's a video showcasing the visuals of Solara One, we invite you and others to enjoy:
https://youtu.be/usPWGlkNvg0

reviews.review_title: Such an amazing game I ever enjoyed
reviews.review_description:
Very immersive and very relaxing 
When you enter in solara one you will feel really in another world 
The developer forgot that it’s an application, they put so much reality in that.

It's a perfect way to relax and be in the present moment. I love looking out the window to see the earth below while I drink my coffee. I'm so looking forward to new features. Thank you for this! I would like to be able to have the option in the space station to sit on the chairs or lay down on the bed to make it a bit more immersive. ❤️

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review. You can actually sit in the chairs as follows: use the thumbstick to  walk up to a chair. Then walk in your physical room to position yourself in the same space as the chair.

To get on the bed, simply use teleport walk onto it and you are all set :)

reviews.review_title: Not sure what there is to do here…
reviews.review_description: I thought there would be more to see. I love the idea, but I don’t think the game play—which isn’t really play—has enough variety to justify a paid app. Maybe Im missing something, but there are similar apps or games with more to see and do. I can use Tripp or Float if I just want to relax.

developer_response.body:
Solara One isn't actually advertised as a game, it's an experience. It is all about immersive exploration, so there’s a lot to discover: from the jungle and Spaceport to interstellar destinations like Earth, Planet Thasos, Saturnia, the Red Giant, and more. You can enjoy guided spacewalks, 5 unique guided crystal meditations, free-fly thruster spacewalks, relax in the lounge or private stateroom, compose music on an interactive music keyboard, and experiment with hand physics-based interactions in zero-G including running tap water, sonic shower, a fully articulating robot doll, etc.

Solara One is all about discovering the cosmos and enjoying what the universe has to offer while exploring both outer space and your inner self at the same time... this is what makes the experience truly unique.

reviews.review_title: The most recent update is superb!!!
reviews.review_description: I rated this four stars and with the recent access to the states room exceeded my expectations.  When the elevator opened up my first thought was "I'm never leaving."  Now if I could only relax on that bed and with the selection of music available, all of my concerns would melt away.  The sink and shower fixtures, the metallophone - sound healing xylophone- are over the top amazing.  I can't wait to see what you're going to do with the arboretum.  This app is headed towards being a top seller and it's because the developer listens to the users and incorporates an artistic approach to technical work .  Get this app... you won't regret it. - Oh and I might add, the choice of music you have is perfect for the meditative experience and it I it really does feel like I've left earth.  I'm in awe!

That was just incredible.  I think I cried a little at one point- my dream to see the space that i know won't come true - kinda did with solara!
I wish that bar in the lounge was real haha!

developer_response.body:
So glad you enjoyed the experience and were able to feel like you really visited outer space.

Stay tuned for an exciting update soon that will include realistic hand physics so you can touch and feel objects as they float around in zero G :)

reviews.review_description:
I use this when I am stressed sometimes just to sit in the space lounge watching the earth slowly move by the wind down.
The latest update has made it so much cooler with the additional "Stateroom" it feels like I also have an apartment or even a hotel to escape in. Anyone who enjoys space, but doesnt always want to do the constant shooting that 99% of the space games require and just wants to chill in space for a bit.  Definitely give this one a try.  I put this game up there with Astra for relaxing space experience, but unlike Astra, this one you can revisit the locations as often as you like.

reviews.review_title: The Atmosphere in this game is awesome..
reviews.review_description:
The GOOD - Seriously though, if you like space and want to come as close as you can to experiencing what it would be like to be up there, this is it. 

SUGGESTION: I think it really add alot to the “0-G” experience if floating objects got affected by just hitting your hands, not having to “grab” it to interact with it. I would sit there for hours just batting a cup back and fourth in 0-G. And maybe make the teleporting to a new space location a bit faster. Other than that, I love it! 🪐🛰️🧑‍🚀

Was pleasantly surprised!

You can visit various places in space. Theres also a space station and jungle, although those are really just “extras.”

The scale of the planets is incredible, and in my experience very few developers take advantage of the ability to make objects appear on grand scale, so if you are someone who enjoys seeing things that look large in VR, you’ll probably enjoy this. Seeing Saturn up close was such a wonderful and soothing experience. 

I highly recommend this app if you like Nature Treks VR or similar travel apps.

Some suggestions: I’d love if there were more information on the planets/locations. Like an optional audio guide with separate parts to learn tidbits and even ancient stories etc.

It would also be nice if some of the stars twinkled and there were shooting stars or something on occasion too.

I would like to be able to open the ceiling up from places like the bedroom/shower to see outside. Otherwise, those rooms don’t seem worth spending time.

Keep up the good work!

Experiences like this are the reason why VR exists. Traveling to distant areas of the universe and just floating with no objective has been the ultimate relaxation. The soundtrack accompanying the incredible visuals is perfect. Please get this if you are in need of some time to just exist alone. Awaiting the arboretum section.

I absolutely love this app, everything about it is so well thought out and expertly crafted. It's about as close to getting to space as I'll ever get and I love it and could spend hours relaxing with all those beautiful celestial and planetary views.  I'm grateful to the developers for bringing us this beautifully designed, immersive, super relaxing experience and I can't wait to see what future updates bring.
Absolutely beautiful. I get on and do spacewalks to decompress after a long day.
Everytime i use it i find it amazing and relaxing. It truly makes you to take a break from your daily routines, situations and issues. God bless the Creator of it!
Calming. Very pretty. Music is very mellow. Glad I bought it.

What an interesting concept. The entire experience so far has been very immersive and relaxing. The xylophone-type instrument was sublime, especially playing along with the amazingly relaxing background music. 

The highlight for me was putting the ship in full gravitational rotation, watching how items responded to artificial gravity, and starting out the window of the space station as earth and the universe spins around me. It actually made me emotional to realize that VR is probably the closest I'll ever get to bring in actual Space. 

Well done! Highly recommended and looking forward to the Arboretum expansion when it's updated.

Absolutely amazing game/experience, I highly recommend this to anybody that bought a vr for unique experiences. The graphics are gorgeous and the atmosphere is relaxing while being immersive. It is one of my favorites and I don't think I can compliment the developer enough for all the work and heart they put into this game
I absolutely love spending time in Solara One, zooming around on the thrusters amongst spectacular nebulae! I wish all apps were up to this high standard. Beautiful and engaging.

Just got my Quest 3 and this was one of the first apps I bought. It's absolutely amazing walking around in the space station, looking outside and seeing all the stars and planets but most of all being out there in space. It actually had me tear up because it was soooo breath taking!

reviews.review_title: The best relaxation app on Quest 3
reviews.review_description: I use this app every single day to relax.  Sitting in the state room over Earth or Saturnia with this app in the background and watching videos is one of the best possible ways to end a day.  The developer created an absolute gem.

reviews.review_title: Awsome, I didn't want to leave!
reviews.review_description: This experience is so much fun and so relaxing, I'll probably visit every day just to chill. Looking forward to the arboretum. My favorite thing to do is cozy up to the window and literally watch the world go by! Haven't been able to explore everything yet but did a couple of spacewalks and using the jet thrusters is thrilling!  The xylophone is a nice touch, I hope you add more sounds to it, like marimba. So glad I bought this app, highly recommend!

Overall, it’s a great app. The visuals genuinely surprised me in a positive way — it feels very calming and definitely has that “wow” effect.  

However, the replay value is quite limited, and after about 1–2 hours you’ve pretty much seen everything. The main reason I didn’t refund it is because I want to support the developers and I hope that more experiences and planets will be added in the future.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review. New content is always in development, stay tuned for updates in the future :)

reviews.review_description:
Great. Excellent. Worth the money. Being on a budget, I would have asked for a refund even though it's on sale as I have gotten refunds on sale items before.  I'm definitely not refunding this one! 

Solara One is wonderful  and the space port definitely has Oblivion starring Tom Cruise vibes. 

The Universe isn't an orderly row of soldiers looking the same in every direction. That's the isotropy model of the Universe which was the old modelling. Jwst was supposed to confirm this modelling. Instead it revealed that the old modelling is all wrong. It isn't an orderly row. It's a jungle. You have planets, stars, galaxies even black holes like TON 618 which has 660 bn solar masses. Black holes usually average about 50 bn solar masses. Etc etc.
JWST found galaxies in regions they shouldn't be. Similar to finding a 25 year old plant in a garden where plants usually grow no more than 3 years. These are leftover galaxies from a previous Universe.
Galaxies usually live about 20 billion years. Light from a galaxy that is 50 billion years old is from a galaxy that's no longer there. The Doppler effect usually makes objects appear smaller as they recede. Dark energy moves faster than light an expands galaxies so they appear larger as they move away. It's like a fun house mirror reflecting an image of something that's no longer there. And because of dark energy, a galaxy that has a light emeanting from it that is 3.8 billion light years is actually 4.3 billion light years away! The Universe isn't a snap shot. It's a process.
The Universe isn't a static expanding inkblot. The Universe moves in a direction, a flow, a grain. Go with the grain. The Great Attractor is pulling galaxies etc towards it.

Well, Solara One is definitely great and it's graphically very attractive about astronomy and great visuals. I used to visit the MacMillan Planetarium in Vancouver with Harold the planetarium projector.
Anyone who is a fan of outer space and NASA will enjoy Solara One. Very beautiful.

This nauseates aliens in UFO spaceships. Every time they approach a planet or a planet and a moon and a sun it's always circles. One circle after another, over and over again. A planet and a moon is like a dish and a coffee saucer on a restaurant table. Always always always with the circle shape, over and and over and over again.

It is great to now be able to take the elevator to visit other parts of the ship… I love the state room living quarters and nice holodeck touch… and I am looking forward to trying the arboretum soon!
This is a great space experience already, and it is lovely to put on the relaxing music and gaze up at the Earth (looks beautiful) from orbit. Everyone can begin to feel like an astronaut from the comfort of their armchair. Keep up the great work and I hope you keep going with this project, into the era of ultra high resolution and wide field of viewrheadset technology coming in the next year or two…would make this experience incredible!
Jeremy

Amazing, I freaking love this, i can spend hours in there so incredible  more planets  more areas, in the station would be fantastic the views of earth  are breathtaking love how the whole experience  works great job! The new elevator and stateroom are amazing. Would  like to go to the moon also ! Teleporting to the surface of some of the planets would be mind blowing!

This experience is designed to allow your body and mind to relax and unwind while exploring spectacular views from space.  You can walk around the Spaceport, high above the clouds of earth, which is awesome to begin with, and then be transported to your Solara One Station in space, with destinations that will make your jaw drop.  The Cyrus Nebula is probably my favourite at this time, and being able to go outside the station in your spacesuit and use your suits thrusters to explore the station up close while soaking up the scenery is amazing.  The music that plays is beautiful as well.  Highly recommend it as a meditative experience.  10/10.

There is something genuinely compelling about applications like this that break out of goal-oriented experiences, moving towards pure simulations that encourage you to immerse yourself in a perspective that simply asks you to suspend your disbelief… and just imagine the reality of your situation! This application takes the participant into a futuristic, progressively more involved state of awe and wonder as you ultimately find yourself weightlessly floating outside the space craft, able to navigate with a spectacular overview like no other! Incredibly well done and as convincing as you dare let go of your ‘life back on Earth’. If I were to suggest a minor enhancement to the devs, I would ask for the classic sunrise/sunset every 45 minutes (a slider to adjust time?) while orbiting Earth with city lights and the occasional appearance of satellites in various orbital paths. The wonderful inclusion of an electronic keyboard is such a pleasure to jam with the various inspiring soundtracks, super nice touch, and of course the Arboretum will surely be great to explore… perhaps some unusual plans indicative of low gravity conditions? Nice bang for the buck in this one❣️Keep up the good work.

reviews.review_title: The closest to the real space visit
reviews.review_description:
I love space and astronomy.
I got this game since the first time I've heard of it.
This app is getting better with every update.
If you love space and want to experience what a walk in a space station feels like, this is for you.
You will not regret it.

Wow where do you start, very relaxing and the attention to detail is of the charts. The taxi is your beginning to an amazing experience, well done to all involved can't wait to see where this goes in the future. Ps can you make it so that when I I'm seated I could sit in the chairs in the lounge,iv found my place to leave the planet

A striking visual immersion into the universe. A relaxed pace that invites you to wander through the heavens.
Solara One is a breath of fresh air in virtual reality: the experience begins on a lush planet, then takes you to a floating space station, and finally, weightless in the vastness of space—all in a breathtaking visual setting.
AC Reality Studios' graphic design is remarkable: colorful nebulae, realistic lighting effects, and meticulous design make each shot a painting to behold.
The soundtrack contributes to the journey: subtle atmospheres, spatial silence, and folds of infinity. However, this contemplative intention comes at the cost of limited interaction: we float, we observe, we breathe, but the action is minimal. The content could be more varied, and the addition of missions or deeper interactions would enhance the experience.
Nevertheless, for those looking for an immersive interlude, a moment of calm after the hustle and bustle of action games, Solara One perfectly fulfills its mission.
As long as you approach it as a journey rather than a game. And in this role, it excels.

I wrote a previous review that wasn’t complimentary. I’d like to fix tha now…

Yet one more example of “Loading” without ever launching. Too bad, I was looking forward to giving this a good review.

Edit: I will, but I won’t have access to my headset for another week or so, in any case I raised my review for your obvious willingness to help.

Edit again.. I changed all the permissions to “yes”, as suggested, and now everything works well. Thank you, devs!

Love the update, told my friend to check it out and they too just purchased it it’s educational and relaxing. Worth buying it. The only thing I really wish is the it was multiplayer with at least 2 people so me and son can explore together. We both have the app and he’s away at college. But it’s still a great app.

My first review on the meta store.
To think this whole experience was created by one person is incredible. This is an absolute steal at the price. This is NOT a game, but a fantastic journey/experience.  A truly wonderful piece of software.
Enjoy
It has amazing views and alot of cool space features you can check out the nebulas are especially peaceful .

This is such a neat experience. I feel like I’m really flouting through space. Exploring the space station. Seeing the earth from space is so cool. 
I love the new upgrades, my state room is lovely. can’t wait to explore the Arboretum. ￼
Beautiful graphics and well designed spaces. Calming music, crystals, and space walks. I love the cleaning mist and taxi ride. The spaceport has so much to explore and learn. I love the meditation balconies. 
I did have one little issue returning from my space walk around the ￼reactor. I didn’t make it back. I was stuck in transport blue sparkly aura. Other than  that everything operates great. Update: it was my headset. I rebooted and everything is working perfectly. Can’t wait for more updates. Thank you Devs! 😁
Just started this app and so far it’s awesome! Very relaxing and great graphics. One question, how do I unlock the space option ? I’ve been all around the space port twice.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review. To finish the Spaceport level, you must walk down the stairs under the giant globe and the teleporter pad will open after about a minute. Good luck 🙂

reviews.review_description: Interesting surroundings , you  can spacewalk and freely fly in space or find your best place on spaceship for relax, i hope they will make more places for visitings in space (blackhole, neutron star and more)  and more specific rooms in spacestation. And online for friends. I love this app , i think about buying for friends

Really makes you feel like you have traveled far out into space. Find a quiet space, put your headset on, load this experience and sit back and feel your racing thoughts and worries dissolve away. Beautiful visuals, fantastic sound design, immersive controls. Looking forward to the arboretum expansion. Recommended for those looking for a meditative experience and those who love travelling the stars.

Great update, I love it!
The new features are excellent. 👌
However, 🤔
I would have loved to see the addition of planets from our solar system, along with some celestial objects like black holes and comets, to make it not only a beautiful and relaxing experience, but also an educational one, with a basic astrophysics learning option. 
That would be a fantastic bonus. 👍

I also have other ideas that might inspire you…
for example, a cinema room with a stunning view of space, a giant screen to share a movie or some music with friends, similar to Bigscreen, including a social area and online discussions.
You could even imagine a virtual office for working, talking, or watching a movie together.

And if one day you add the French language, that would be perfect!
But I wouldn’t dare ask… lol

developer_response.body: So glad you enjoyed the experience. Thanks for your review and suggestions, will keep them in mind for sure 😊

reviews.review_description:
Revised 25/12/2025 - 4.5 stars
 The space station immersion is excellent,  then flying around the spaceship near earth,  quite stunning.  With the recent resolution update the planets and nebula are quite immersive also.

The new living  quarters are such good quality.   Looking forward to the holo-forest. 
 
It's the price of a cinema ticket and probably the same value.  Well worth it to come back to and chill now and again

reviews.review_title: So beautiful I can't explain experience
reviews.review_description:
I congratulate ur hard work over this solara one app..
I'm unexplainable..
I recommend that continue same orbits with  moon ,Jupiter,Mars  saturn ...and if possible remaining planets..
Try to show nebulas galaxies as a separate tour 
And also constellation s route in separate maps...🎉🎉
This app is unbelievable and I'm out of my mind as I I'm going through orbits of earth..
If possible show different orbits of earth so that we can cover all planets for example australia, europe ,russia south america...
Kindly without clouds nd with more clarity of specific places marked on earth example statue of liberty,tajmahal,china wall,Eiffel tower 🗼 like as options to click those places so that it will be very nice to look our lovely places.

I take my hat off to the developer of this project my mind has totally been blown away.This is probably going to save my sanity in this awful world we live in. I will be coming here every day it is the best £7.99 I have spent in my entire life. Thank you!

great to have a home place to sleep in space now because the taxiboy is not really talky to take it every day back to earth (maybe on the route lets us see with the taxi flying where we are living in space not just a carjump ;-))
also have a kitchen its great so i'm waiting to cook something great for you all but need a garden to pick the food there so build a great arboretum is on for the next thats should be fine for me.

Thank you for great experience!
Regards to developers.

As an avid megalophilia enjoyer, this gave me two hits. The asteroid belt and the red moon gave me visceral reactions. Big props for the devs for those.

My gripes with this experience are the fact that the stellar objects presented here just aren't as gigantic as they are supposed to be. That obviously isn't realistically achievable with current technology.

My second gripe is tied to my previous issue: the movement of those objects in relation to your view. Realistically when you move in space, you really can't observe any displacement of stellar objects because of the miniscule distance you've traveled. I shouldn't be able to see the moon or THE SUN  moving in relation to me when I travel few measely kilometers in any direction. They should stay perfectly still in relation to my view.

My last gripe: please add audio options. I want to be in almost perfect silence when I'm floating in outer space. The 'meditative' music imo somewhat ruins the experience.

Overall my issues are minor and the experience was cool. I love outer space and appreciate the effort of the devs.

EDIT: As the dev pointed out, audio options were hidden not in options but in the spacewalk menu :D good that it exists but intuitively the options should probably be in the, well, options menu.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback, it will be kept in mind for sure.

In terms of the scale of the objects and seeing parallax of stellar objects when moving, congrats on noticing this - you have a keen eye. However, these are technological limitations of the quest hardware itself, we have pushed the GPU to its limits already. Basically the depth map used to render images on the GPU only has so many bits so objects cannot truly be an infinite distance away...

In regards to turning off the music, this can already be done with the left controller holographic menu, just be sure to turn it off before starting the spacewalk :)

Stay tuned for more exciting updates! :)

reviews.review_description: This is the most closest experience for space traveling I have ever seen. Visit the earth orbit in a space ship, and other awesome places of the outer space like an asteriod cluster, Saturnalia, a colosal nebula, and other incredible places that will give you a special feeling.

The new graphics updates really make the visuals pop in the Quest headset. It's a really nice chill taking the guided spacewalks around the different settings.  Great work by the developer. Recommended
Top👍👍👍
Brink Traveler in space, mind blowing stuff, with a poor UI tho. A must buy for sure. Tried a lot of space VR app and it's one of the best on quest.

Wow!  This is even better than I hoped.  I haven't even come close to exploring it all yet because I just like to drift in each setting and don't want to leave.  The way the "scenes" very slowly morphs as the spaceship turns makes me just want to see more and more unfold.  The background mood music is peaceful as well. The first entry sections are fun to play in and have a huge wow factor as well.  

Meta needs to set up opportunities for the public to experience this!  They would sell hundreds of thousands of the Quest headsets if the public knew about this Solara experience alone.  People that have not tried Quest still think VR is just video games.  This is an entirely more bankable level in my humble opinion. Most Quest owners are still gamers first hence, this probably doesn't sell as many as it would otherwise if the message actually got out.

I really enjoyed the trip to space and especially the meditation stones. Could be nice with different meditation music and other “exercises” depending on the colour of the stone. I tried it an hour before bedtime and I feel a sleep smoothly.
The feling when you are outside the ship is amazing - gazing at the stars.
I wish though you could do some more things apart from just floating around.
Looking forward to see what comes next - some doors were closed because of “developing”
But all in all a nice and relaxing experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review. Be sure to download the latest version, the latest update now includes a functional elevator with more rooms to explore on the space station :)

reviews.review_description: Amazing work..love that project

First off, I want to say - if you want to blow things up, or shoot an endless steam of space zombies - this game is not for you.
 
However, if you want your mind blown by amazing space walks and exploring incredible space vistas, this IS the experience you have been looking for.
 
There are a few “levels” to the game. You start on Earth, and make your way up to a spaceport which has some cool interaction points and impressive cloudscapes. But for me, the game really impressed the most when I arrived at the space station - I felt like I was really in orbit around the earth. Looking out of the windows and then going on a spacewalk - I might as well have been an astronaut on the ISS. The jet packs are a lot of fun too and let you explore where the guided space walks do not.
 
And then I started warping to other destinations and was blown away all over again. Asteroid fields, nebulae, red giant systems and so many more. It felt like Star Trek TNG (yes, I'm aging myself! 😂) only with better graphics and no Klingons trying to kill me.
 
Bottom line - if you want an app to dazzle your friends with amazing space graphics, or you have always wanted to visit the distant reaches of the galaxy and explore them - then treat yourself to Solara One. It will be the best $10 you have ever spent.
Not enough to do.  Great visuals but not worth the price.  Would be nice if they included the solar system

developer_response.body: Did you have time to explore the jungle, the spaceport, the space station, and all 8 star systems? That's 10 things to do right there ;)

reviews.review_description:
I sometimes get stressed playing shooter games so it’s nice to mix it up by chillin with apps like Solara One. If you like Brink Traveler, Ocean Rift, or Nature Treks you might like this one. 

I love sci-fi stuff. Solara One is the closest I’ll get to imagining myself as a space traveler. They did a great job with clouds layers and atmospheric scenes. 
I have never experienced a better simulation of floating above the earth as it slowly passes by you. I also appreciate it when you travel in and out of the Solara One spacecraft you’re still in the same orbital position of earth - maintaining continuity and credibility. 

Minor gripes:
The main menu is too sensitive and you can click on buttons accidentally

It’s hard to hold the crystals for long periods of time and not very relaxing. 
Maybe make it a wristband you could wear or something. 

You can tell the space skybox has been reused in several Destinations. 

Wish there was more jungle stuff. Also I tried climbing into the car once the droid asked me to climb in. Turns out you need to step on circle to be transported. 

Other than that… Great experience.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. The crystal grab behavior was fixed in the latest patch :) Stay tuned for other updates!

reviews.review_description: So calming and tranquil it felt like going on a cruise with no one else around. A dream.
This is a cool and interesting piece of work. I can’t wait to see what’s coming next.

As an astronomer and wannabe astronaut can I thank you for this incredible experience. I can spend a long time literally just space walking and taking in the views.

 Thank you also for making the stars, constellations and deep sky objects true to reality. 

All I could ask for is more scenarios, including an accurate lunar surface and other planets from our solar system. A closer detailed view of Earth too please !!

Thanks again Devs for this gem of an experience.

The graphics are amazing, making losing yourself in these environments astoundingly easy. The expanses are breathtaking, and on each new space walk you can't help but have a smile on your face as you drift and explore these great scenes whilst getting caught up in the ambience and soothing music.
This application does exactly what's promised and all with very well done AAA design.

Especially for the price, this is definitely an application you will love having if you want a relaxing escape and/ or love space.
I really enjoy this game. I have a few other relaxing games, but this is my favorite.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review, glad you are enjoying it ☺️

reviews.review_title: Nice visual, not much to do.
reviews.review_description:
Nice to get around and pretty things to stop and look at, but as the space station lounge was in spin, would love to have coriolis effects on the things in the room, and have gravity and physics adjust as you change the spin speed. 
Jet packing around is cool, but again i tried to grab on to the spinning ring and this just isn’t modelled. 
Hope maybe this gets added in later versions?

developer_response.body: Centripetal acceleration on space lounge objects is coming in the next release. Stay tuned :)

reviews.review_description: This is a wonderful experience. Floating in the vastness of endless space, I felt so small.

Solara One has become one of my favourite apps on the Quest. Its scope is quite modest, but it does what it does very well and with quite an unusual spin on meditation apps. I often plan to spend just a few minutes in it, and after half an hour have to drag myself reluctantly away. It really is that immersive - I can almost feel that I'm really on the space station. And it's better still with Quest Games Optimiser, of course.

Here are some more specific comments. The critical ones are really just minor nit-picks as overall this is a very positive experience.

The initial environment, the jungle, isn't as impressive as the others and feels rather tacked on. Similarly, the robot-driven flying car is also rather out of keeping with the atmosphere. I always just start at the spaceport.

The spaceport is really well done, and I like the use of displays and videos to create both realism and a sense of excitement about the forthcoming trip. The clouds look really impressive, but the effect is marred by the fact that they seem to be just one texture repeated many times. It would be much more immersive if there could be some variety in the textures.

The space station itself is better still. I really like the fact that each location you can warp to has a quite different feel to it. Some of the textures on the interior are a bit simple though, and the pressable buttons are quite primitive-looking. I'd really like to be able to reverse the rotation, and also for the rotation speed to be saved from one session to the next (I like to slow it down, but it's annoying having to do that every time!).

The views out of the window are spectacular. The only flaw is that the planets all seem to have impossibly gigantic moons that are so close they're almost touching the planet, which don't look at all believable.

Obviously I'd love to see some more locations to warp to and more rooms in the space station, but I believe they're coming!

1: so the main place is inside a rotating structure as in sci-fi movies for gravity - you can adjust the rotation speed, but no objects inside the structure are affected by any gravity. 
2: "you" on the other hand, are not weightless(maybe its best not to be), but you cannot even select "sitting/standing" using right thumb button, often being too low when sitting. (add a simple stand/sit toggle on right thumbstick)
3: why don't you stick to standard UI/UX ? - the right game settings button should be used for  menu - not "X" 
4:  make the menu better, with controls on one page, you have too many "pages" on it.
5: why have people to actively hold the crystal all the time? - that's just annoying, you could "place a crystal in a holder" to use  it,  or even have it be the only object type that has "toggle" on it's grip.
Keep up the good work - more stars are not far away.

developer_response.body:
Greetings Earthling! Please update to the latest version for these updates:

The space station objects now accelerate due to the ring rotation speed .

The crystal grab behavior has been modified to continue without having to continuously grab it.

Player height can be adjusted in the settings (this has always been available)

Stay tuned for other exciting updates. In the meantime feel free to checkout BMFVR's review here https://youtu.be/usPWGlkNvg0
